authSessionLocalStorageTransitions.valSettings004: No auth/session localStorage keys were present; invalid save did not persist config overrides and left focus on settingsSheetId.
authSessionLocalStorageTransitions.valSettings007: Saved config override contained the public sheet ID with blank oauthClientId; no auth/session localStorage keys were present before or after opening the discovery wizard.

notes: Both assertions were tested in a dedicated signed-out browser session with fresh app storage at the start of each flow.
No authenticated Google session was used or imported.

assertions.title: Invalid sheet input blocks save when OAuth bootstrap is unavailable

steps.action: Open Settings from the signed-out surface in a fresh session, clear the OAuth Client ID field on Setup, switch to Sheet, enter `not-a-sheet`, and click `Save & reload`.
steps.expected: Settings stays open, focuses the Sheet tab/input, and shows remediation instead of silently reloading away.
steps.observed: Settings remained open on the Sheet tab at `http://localhost:8080/`, the active element stayed `settingsSheetId`, and an inline `settingsFormError` message appeared: 'Paste your spreadsheet’s full URL from the browser bar, or the Sheet ID only (the long id between /d/ and /edit).'

steps.action: Inspect browser storage after the invalid save attempt.
steps.expected: The invalid submission should not create a usable OAuth bootstrap path or persist the bad sheet value as a successful save.
steps.observed: No auth/session localStorage keys existed and `command_center_config_overrides` was not persisted for the invalid submission.

evidence.network: No relevant network request was required for validation; the app stayed on the same URL and handled the invalid sheet input in-modal.

assertions.title: Discovery-focused settings entry points land on the discovery setup surface

steps.action: Reset browser storage, save the public sheet ID with blank OAuth Client ID, land on the signed-out read-only dashboard, and click the dashboard CTA `Open discovery setup wizard`.
steps.expected: The dashboard/setup CTA opens a visible discovery setup surface for the current flow.
steps.observed: The discovery wizard opened over the dashboard with visible setup chrome including `Close wizard`, step buttons (`Status`, `Path`, `Config`, `Server`, `Tunnel`, `Relay`, `Test`, `Done`), and a `Re-scan` action.

steps.action: Close the wizard, open `Settings and setup`, switch to the `Discovery` tab, and click `Open discovery setup`.
steps.expected: The Settings discovery CTA lands on the discovery setup surface instead of leaving the user on the tab or dead-ending.
steps.observed: The Settings CTA again opened the discovery wizard overlay on the same signed-out dashboard, replacing the modal with the visible discovery setup surface.

evidence.network: No navigation or discovery POST was required; both entry points opened the discovery setup wizard in place on `http://localhost:8080/`.

summary: Retested 2 signed-out security-config settings assertions. VAL-SETTINGS-004 passed because invalid sheet input kept Settings open on the Sheet tab with visible remediation and focus on the sheet field. VAL-SETTINGS-007 passed because both the dashboard CTA and the Settings Discovery CTA visibly opened the discovery setup wizard.
